message.content: Adversaries may exploit private data from threat intelligence vendors to gather actionable information for targeting. These vendors offer paid feeds or portals with more detailed data than publicly available, including trends on breaches, target industries, attribution claims, and successful TTPs/countermeasures. Even with redacted sensitive details, adversaries can use this information to enhance their campaigns. They may seek insights on their own activities or those of other threat actors with similar objectives. This intelligence can aid in reconnaissance, developing or obtaining capabilities, and gaining initial access through methods like exploiting public-facing applications or external remote services. Mitigation strategies include restricting access to threat intelligence data, monitoring for unauthorized access, and employing robust security measures to protect sensitive information.
